anchors.claim: The bundle carries its source revision identifier.
One binary selects CLI, IDE, remote, SDK, MCP, GitHub Action, and other entrypoint identities from an environment discriminator.
The deep-link handler rejects trailing arguments after the URI as possible argument injection.
A proxy authentication helper sourced from project/local settings is skipped before workspace trust is accepted.
Local IPC checks require a 0700 socket directory.
Managed policy can make non-managed permission rules ineffective.
Policy can disable bypass-permissions mode.
A subprocess hardening mode forces default permissions unless tools are explicitly allowed.
Managed deployments can fail startup when a required sandbox is unavailable.
Configuration can make the per-command sandbox-disable parameter ineffective.
Sandboxed Bash commands can be auto-allowed by an explicit setting.
A macOS compatibility option explicitly weakens network isolation.
Nested execution has a separate explicit weaker-sandbox compatibility option.
The hook bus includes pre/post tool, permission, session, subagent, compaction, worktree, and file/config lifecycle events.
The central turn engine is implemented as an asynchronous generator.
A built-in registry assembles tool implementations before mode and feature filtering.
Individual tool calls pass through a shared asynchronous execution pipeline.
The bundle preserves an original filename for Bash read-only validation logic.
Plugin directories can be loaded while suppressing their MCP contribution.
Plugin monitor scripts execute unsandboxed at the same trust tier as hooks.
anchors.needle: unsandboxed, same trust tier as hooks

anchors.claim: The plugin inventory includes skills, agents, hooks, MCP, LSP, output styles, and channels.
MCP servers are loaded from a typed settings record.
Strict MCP mode excludes sources other than the explicitly supplied configuration.
MCP configuration recognizes stdio, SSE, IDE-SSE, HTTP, WebSocket, and SDK transport families.
Project-scoped MCP approvals are persisted separately from discovery.
The engine can push a replacement slash-command list when skills are discovered mid-session.
Tool names support configurable single-hop aliases before resolution.
An optional prompt fragment is propagated to Task subagents and nested subagents.
Subagent startup is observable through the hook lifecycle.
Turn completion records pending background-agent and workflow counts.
The session idle signal is emitted only after held-back results flush and the background-agent loop exits.
Remote Control can start automatically for each session.
Peer-machine isolation can require explicit approval before cross-machine messages.
Automatic memory reads and writes are independently configurable.
A custom auto-memory directory is ignored when supplied by checked-in project settings.
Compaction emits separate progress and completed-boundary events.
Direct API-key authentication is supported.
A shell helper can supply the API key.
The macOS credential path invokes the system keychain command.
Claude.ai OAuth authorization uses an explicit endpoint constant.
Amazon Bedrock is an alternate provider route.
Google Vertex AI is an alternate provider route.
Microsoft Foundry is an alternate provider route.
First-party event logging batches to a versioned API path.
A direct telemetry disable switch exists alongside other observability controls.
Nonessential outbound traffic can be disabled with an environment control.
Native releases are fetched from Anthropic's Claude Code release origin.
An external SessionStore adapter has a bounded load path.
Local persistence distinguishes session transcript content.
The HTTP layer distinguishes Anthropic-operated endpoints from external HTTP destinations.
The automatic permission classifier treats attempts to tunnel around a denial as bypass behavior.
